race.name: Furusjøen Rundt
race.tagline: Forty-five kilometers circling Furusjøen in the Rondane foothills — a February classic that doubles as your Birken qualifier and a Ski Classics Challengers test piece.

vitals.aid_stations: Feed stations around the lake circuit

climate.description: February at 900m altitude in the Rondane foothills means deep winter. Cold, stable conditions are typical. Temperatures generally -15°C to -5°C. Snow is highly reliable at this altitude and time of year. Short daylight hours in early February.
challenges: Deep cold in February at mountain altitude
Wind exposure on open sections around the lake
Short February daylight — early start required

course.primary: Loop course circling Furusjøen lake in the foothills of the Rondane mountain range. The circuit follows classic tracks through forest and open mountain terrain, with views toward the Rondane peaks. Rolling terrain with moderate climbs as the course traces the lake's perimeter.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through mountain terrain
course.grooming: Good quality, Ski Classics Challengers standard
features: Views toward the Rondane National Park peaks
Mix of forest and open mountain terrain

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Strong snow reliability at mountain altitude. Scenic Rondane views lift the scenery score. Primary value as Birkebeinerrennet seeding race — many participants use it to qualify. Limited field size and primarily Norwegian participation.

history.summary: A 45km classic race circling Furusjøen lake in Norway's Innlandet region, in the foothills of the Rondane mountain range. Part of the Ski Classics Challengers series, it also functions as a seeding race for the prestigious Birkebeinerrennet. The race attracts around 500 participants, many of whom are training and qualifying for the Birken.
notable_facts: Functions as a Birkebeinerrennet seeding/qualifying race
Located in the foothills of Rondane, one of Norway's oldest national parks
Part of the Ski Classics Challengers series
Loop format around Furusjøen lake is unusual for Norwegian ski marathons
~517 participants reflects its role as a qualifier rather than destination race

videos.title: Highlights from Furusjøen Rundt-Rennet 2026
videos.description:
Highlights from Furusjøen Rundt-Rennet 2026

The Ski Classics Challengers event, Furusjøen Rundt-Rennet, took place last Saturday (February 14) and featured a 45km long-distance ski race in classic technique in Kvam, Norway. Watch the highlights here!

Video: Daniel Strandheim
videos.curation_reason: Recent highlights from the 2026 Ski Classics Challengers 45km classic technique race showing race coverage and course layout.

videos.title: Proud over our beautiful mountains
videos.description:
The community are now preparing for this years event.
We are proud over our beautiful mountains and are looking forward to great you all a warm welcome to Furusjoen Rundt MTB July 22nd. 
Welcome! 😍🚴🏻‍♂️

videos.title: Welcome to Kvam, Lillehammer and Norway - MTB Marathon riders!
videos.description:
Welcome to 2019 UEC MTB Marathon European Championships for Elite and Masters. 

Welcome to Lillehammer and Kvam! 

In the Lillehammer region, you can explore the very best of Norwegian countryside – from high mountains to forest-clad hills and cultural landscapes. Your next adventure is never far away.

The valley north of Lillehammer, Gudbrandsdalen, you will find the tiny village of Kvam. 
A fantastic area with hundreds of kilometeres of tracks all year long from skiing, biking and hiking. MTB race in adventurous environment. 

The ciricuit is in the mountains with paths and challenges of varying degrees of difficulty, rapid forest trails to tough downhill and climbs.

videos.title: Official Aftermovie 2019  UEC MTB Marathon European Championships | Furusjøen Rundt-rittet
videos.description:
Thank you - Merci beaucoup - Grazie mille - Vielen Dank - Muchas gracias

Vi er stolte av vårt flotte fjell, og vi takker dere alle for besøket. 
Takk til UEC og Terreng Maraton Europamesterskapet. 
Takk til utøvere, støtteapparat, frivillige og publikum. 
Vi er stolte av dere! 

We are proud of our beautiful mountain, and we thank you all for your visit 
Thanks to UEC and the MTB Marathon European Championship. 
Thanks to practitioners, support staff, volunteers and the public. 
We are proud of you!
